differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) presenting with catastrophic manifestations
Infectious pneumonia with secondary reactive nephrotic syndrome
next_steps: Perform diagnostic serology including ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, C3/C4 levels, and antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I)
Perform renal biopsy to distinguish between glomerulonephritis types and assess chronicity
Initiate systemic anticoagulation with heparin, given bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism
Send serum anti-PLA2R antibody and anti-GBM antibody titers
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood/sputum culture results
Monitor renal function, electrolytes, and coagulation parameters daily
